title: Inside the Longevity Boom: Where Science Ends and Fantasy Begins
description: The longevity sector attracted $8.49B in 2024 (a 220% jump) and is projected to reach $8T by 2030 per UBS, as USC researcher Valter Longo warns people are living longer but sicker.

title: NIA ITP March 2026 Results: Zero Compounds Extended Lifespan
description: The NIH Interventions Testing Program released March 2026 results showing zero compounds extended lifespan; pioglitazone shortened female lifespan by 6.2% after 20 years of testing.

title: Global Longevity Federation 2026 Opens in Rome
description: Global experts convene in Rome March 23-24 to discuss the future of longevity, healthy aging, and regenerative medicine at the Global Longevity Federation hybrid event.

title: LA Times: Longevity Industry Turns Aging into an Investable Market
description: Biotech, AI diagnostics, and venture capital are converging across Southern California to make longevity a scalable growth frontier, shifting from boutique wellness to investable healthtech.

title: Biotech Stocks Surge as Longevity Investment Doubles to $8.49B
description: Private longevity investment more than doubled in 2025, hitting $8.49B across 325 deals, as the FDA approved 46 new drugs including the first class labeled potential longevity therapeutics.

title: When Longevity Data Grows Up
description: Longevity.Technology is shifting from observing the field to structuring it, while UT Health San Antonio preps a $38M human trial testing three existing medicines for extending healthy lifespan.

title: Popular Anti-Aging Senolytic Combo D+Q Causes Brain Myelin Damage in Mice
description: Landmark PNAS study from the Crocker Lab finds dasatinib+quercetin (D+Q), a widely used senolytic combo, triggers myelin loss in the brain of both young and aged mice, raising safety concerns.

title: mRNA Lipid Nanoparticle Fights Tau Aggregation in Alzheimer's Disease
description: A lipid nanoparticle delivers TRIM11 mRNA to neurons, dissolving tau aggregates and restoring cognition in Alzheimer's mouse models with effects lasting three months.

title: WHOOP Enters $34.5M ARPA-H-Backed Bid to Quantify Aging
description: Wearable company WHOOP joins an ARPA-H-backed consortium in a $34.5M program to develop tools for quantifying biological aging through continuous health monitoring.

title: Longevity Biotech Market Valued at $12.5B, Projected to Hit $45.8B by 2033
description: The global longevity biotech market is valued at $12.5B in 2026 and projected to reach $45.8B by 2033, growing at a CAGR of 20.4% driven by AI-optimized drug discovery pipelines.

title: Longevity Innovation Forum: Aging Drug v1 Targets Disease, v2 Will Target Aging Itself
description: At the Longevity Innovation Forum in San Diego, panelists discussed how first-gen aging drugs target age-related diseases while the next generation will target the aging process directly.

title: Vitamin C Inhibits ACSL4 to Alleviate Ferro-Aging in Primates
description: Steve Horvath highlights March 2026 as a strong month for everyday supplement research, noting new findings on Vitamin C's role in inhibiting ferroptosis-related aging pathways in primates.

title: 11 Anti-Aging Biotech Companies Leading Longevity in 2026
description: Labiotech profiles 11 leading anti-aging companies including Altos Labs ($3B), Retro Biosciences, Cambrian Bio ($30.8M ARPA-H award), and others advancing epigenetic reprogramming and gene therapy.

title: Shingles Vaccine Drastically Cuts Risk of Serious Cardiac Events
description: New data presented at ACC shows the shingles vaccine significantly reduces the risk of serious cardiac events, adding to the evidence linking infection control to healthy aging.

title: Wilbe Longevity Build Week: Global Sprint for Longevity Ventures
description: A fully online sprint for researchers and builders exploring how longevity science can become real ventures, covering cellular reprogramming, aging biomarkers, and AI-driven aging biology.

title: Top Longevity Startups by Fundraising: Altos Labs Leads at $3B
description: The five most funded longevity startups (Altos Labs, Oura, Retro Biosciences, BioSplice, Insilico Medicine) account for roughly $6.9B, with Retro's $1B Series A as the largest single round.

title: The Senolytics Pipeline in 2026: Where the Science Still Looks Strong
description: A Nature Aging study compared 21 senolytic agents, finding ABT263 and ARV825 most effective. The field is narrowing toward more selective, biomarker-driven approaches.

title: Daily Multivitamins Slow Biological Aging by 4 Months in Clinical Trial
description: The COSMOS trial found daily multivitamins slowed all five epigenetic aging clocks by about 4 months over 2 years, with greatest benefits for those biologically older than their actual age.

title: US Government Funds $144M PROSPR Program for First Human Aging Trials
description: ARPA-H launches PROSPR, a $144M program for the first human clinical trials targeting aging directly, featuring rapamycin-based approaches and marking a paradigm shift in longevity medicine.

title: Juvenescence Advances Aging Drug to Phase 2 Trial
description: Juvenescence moves its aging drug candidate into Phase 2 clinical trials, part of a growing wave of longevity therapeutics entering mid-stage human testing in 2026.

title: Wearable Startup Temple Secures $54M for Brain Monitoring
description: Temple raises $54M to develop wearable brain monitoring technology, expanding the intersection of neuroscience and longevity diagnostics.

title: Annovis CEO Eyes Spring Breakthrough for Alzheimer's Drug
description: Annovis Bio's CEO signals a potential spring breakthrough for their Alzheimer's drug candidate, as the company advances its pipeline targeting neurodegeneration.

title: Scientists Reveal Why a Popular Anti-Aging Compound May Also Fuel Cancer
description: Polyamines, celebrated for boosting cellular cleanup and healthy aging, are found to also turbocharge cancer growth, revealing a hidden double life for these longevity molecules.

title: Rejuvenation Roundup February 2026: Senolytic Mitochondrial Stress Mechanism
description: Nature Aging researchers show mitochondrial stress is key to senolytic effectiveness, while Aging Cell reports on restoring natural killer cell function to combat immune aging.

title: Brown University Wins $22M Federal Award to Study Anti-Aging Treatment
description: Brown and Rochester researchers will test whether an HIV drug can quiet chronic DNA-triggered inflammation to slow human aging, in the first major trial targeting the 'dark genome' mechanism.
